name: Cartel detection via co-bidding pattern analysis (CABA OCDS)

dataset.note: Dataset contains only awarded bids (status=adjudicada). Win-rate analysis not possible — all bids are winners. Analysis focuses on co-award patterns and market concentration.

methodology: Co-award pair analysis: contractors repeatedly awarded lots within the same procedures
Triad cluster detection: groups of 3+ firms co-awarded across multiple procedures
Hub-spoke analysis: firms that serve as central nodes connecting many co-awardees
Market concentration by procurement unit: bidder/procedure ratio
Single-bidder procedure detection for high-value contracts

clusters.label: Surgical Supplies Triad
clusters.pattern: These three surgical supply firms appear together in 7 procedures and in pairwise combinations across 15+ hospital procurement processes. J&J and Cirugia Argentina co-appear most frequently (12 procedures). All three are multinational or large domestic surgical supply firms splitting lot awards within the same tenders.
cartel_indicators: Repeated co-appearance across multiple procurement units (410, 434, 418, 426, 425, 435)
Complementary product lines suggest coordinated lot-splitting rather than genuine competition
Covidien is a Medtronic subsidiary — potential coordinated bidding with J&J on surgical consumables

clusters.label: Laboratory Reagents Hub (Quimica Cordoba Network)

members.name: ERNESTO VAN ROSSUM Y COMPAÑIA S.R.L.

clusters.pattern: Quimica Cordoba operates as a hub node connecting 25 distinct co-awardees across 23 procedures. The top 7 partners each share 7-13 procedures. This is the densest co-award network in the dataset.
cartel_indicators: Hub-spoke topology: one central firm coordinates with a rotating set of partners
13 shared procedures between QC and Van Rossum is extremely high for this market
Network spans multiple procurement units — not explained by product specialization alone
Triad QC + Van Rossum + Medi Sistem appears in 6 procedures together

clusters.label: Pharmaceutical Distribution Cluster
clusters.pattern: Richet and Alpha Medical co-appear in 14 procedures — the highest pair frequency in the dataset. Alpha Medical also co-appears with Fresenius Kabi (9), Drogueria Garzon (9), and several others. Cluster of 6 pharmaceutical firms with dense interconnections.
cartel_indicators: 14 shared procedures between Richet and Alpha Medical is the single highest co-bidding pair
Alpha Medical appears as a secondary hub connecting Richet, Fresenius, Garzon, Biofarma
Multiple triads detected within this cluster (Richet+Biofarma+Alpha: 4 shared, Richet+Garzon+Alpha: 4 shared)

clusters.label: Drogueria Distribution Network
clusters.pattern: Drogueria Martorani is a hub connecting 4 other pharmaceutical distributors. Martorani and Farmatec co-appear in 11 procedures ($79M combined). The drogueria cluster also intersects with the surgical triad — Martorani co-appears with J&J in 8 procedures.
cartel_indicators: 11 co-appearances between two droguerias in the same tender processes
Cross-cluster connection: Martorani bridges the drogueria network to the surgical supplies triad
Martorani appears in procedures with the most co-awardees (14-awardee procedure 420-0320-LPU22)

clusters.label: Chemical Reagents Cluster
clusters.pattern: Quimica Erovne is the second-highest hub after Quimica Cordoba, co-appearing with Medica Tec and Bioartis in 5 procedures as a triad, and with Bioquimica in 6. This cluster also overlaps with the Quimica Cordoba network.
cartel_indicators: Triad Erovne + Medica Tec + Bioartis co-appears in 5 procedures
Overlaps with QC hub network — potential super-cluster of lab chemical suppliers
Erovne has $168M in awards across 34 procedures, making it a major market player

most_concentrated_units.note: Only 8 bidders for 16 procedures — highest concentration
Tech procurement — contains 3 of top 10 single-bidder high-value contracts

market_concentration.single_bidder_rate: 78.2% of procedures (1374/1757) have only one awardee

anomalies.detail: Top recipient with $5.3B in total awards has NO CUIT on record. All awards from a single procedure (101-1835-LPU21) with duplicate bid entries — possible data quality issue or obfuscation.
Individual persons (not companies) winning government procurement contracts across multiple procedures. Poggi and Lalanne also appear in the Quimica Cordoba network as co-awardees.
A single individual awarded $3.1 billion ARS with no competing bids. This is the largest single-bidder contract in the dataset by a factor of 4x.

summary.highest_co_bidding_pair: LABORATORIOS RICHET + ALPHA MEDICAL GROUP (14 shared procedures)
summary.densest_network: QUIMICA CORDOBA hub (25 co-awardees across 23 procedures)
summary.largest_triad: J&J + COVIDIEN + CIRUGIA ARGENTINA (7 shared procedures, $54M combined)
summary.key_insight: The CABA procurement market shows two distinct patterns: (1) Dense co-award networks in medical/laboratory supplies suggesting coordinated lot-splitting among regular participants, and (2) High-value single-bidder contracts in technology and construction suggesting barriers to entry or specification tailoring. 78% of procedures have only one awardee.
